migrationWindow.condition: Supabase REST and Storage APIs must be accessible and no longer return 402 before legacy data migration starts

goal: Maximize long-term free-tier or near-free operation by separating hosting, primary database, media storage, and logs so one quota overrun does not block the whole service.

decisionUpdate.reason: The user is willing to accept a larger development scope for a cheaper long-term architecture. The existing CockroachDB cluster has already reached its monthly Request Unit limit, so CockroachDB Basic should not remain the primary free-production recommendation without throttling, caching, and quota alerts.

targetMonthlyBaselineUsd.note: Use Cloudflare Workers Paid as the low fixed production baseline when daily Free-plan limits or reliability risk matter. R2 and D1 can remain inside included/free allowances while usage is small.

options.name: Vercel Hobby + CockroachDB Basic + Cloudflare R2

services.logs: CockroachDB initially, then D1 or Turso if log volume grows

freeQuotaReference.database: 10 GiB storage free per month and 50 million RUs per month
freeQuotaReference.media: 10 GB-month storage, 1M Class A operations, 10M Class B operations, free egress

pros: Large free primary DB storage among practical SQL options
Keeps a relational SQL model closer to Postgres than SQLite-only options
Breaks the Supabase Storage quota failure chain
risks: CockroachDB is not identical to PostgreSQL and needs compatibility tests
Request-unit usage can rise without indexes and caching
Supabase REST-specific code must be replaced with a provider layer
The current CulturePeople CockroachDB cluster has already shown monthly RU exhaustion, so this path needs throttling, caching, and RU alerts before it can be trusted as primary

options.name: Vercel Hobby + Turso Free + Cloudflare R2

services.logs: Turso with retention or Cloudflare D1

freeQuotaReference.database: 5 GB total storage, 500M monthly rows read, 10M monthly rows written
freeQuotaReference.media: 10 GB-month storage, 1M Class A operations, 10M Class B operations, free egress

pros: Good fit for article CMS if SQLite constraints are acceptable
risks: SQLite/libSQL migration requires deeper query rewrites
Full scans can consume row-read quota quickly

options.name: Cloudflare Workers/Pages + D1 + R2

services.hosting: Cloudflare Workers or Pages Functions via OpenNext
services.logs: Cloudflare D1 with daily summaries and raw-log retention

freeQuotaReference.databaseFree: 5 GB total storage, 5M rows read per day, 100k rows written per day
freeQuotaReference.databaseWorkersPaid: 5 GB storage included before overage, 25B rows read per month, 50M rows written per month
freeQuotaReference.media: 10 GB-month storage, 1M Class A operations, 10M Class B operations, free egress
freeQuotaReference.runtimeWorkersPaid: About $5/month baseline, 10M Worker requests/month included, 30M CPU milliseconds/month included

pros: D1 and R2 have no egress charge
Good long-term separation if app is adapted to Workers
Lowest predictable long-term cost when larger development work is acceptable
risks: Current Next.js API routes may require significant changes
Node-only runtime dependencies must be removed from Worker paths or isolated into offline jobs
D1 is SQLite-based, so Postgres/Supabase-specific SQL assumptions must be rewritten

options.name: Vercel Hobby + Supabase DB Free + Cloudflare R2
pros: Fixes the immediate Supabase Storage quota failure
risks: Not aligned with the target new-DB direction
Future Supabase DB quota issues can still block service

options.name: Vercel Hobby + Aiven PostgreSQL Developer + Cloudflare R2

services.logs: PostgreSQL with retention, or D1 for cheap history

paidCostReference.media: R2 free while under 10 GB-month Standard storage
paidCostReference.hosting: Vercel Hobby if policy and usage fit

pros: Much smaller app rewrite than D1
risks: Still relies on Vercel unless hosting is moved later
Developer tiers are cheaper but not equivalent to full production/SLA database plans
PostgreSQL storage and backup policy must be verified before final migration

preMay18Preparation: Create Cloudflare D1 database and R2 bucket for staging
Build DB and storage provider abstractions
Create import scripts that accept exported Supabase JSON/CSV and Storage manifests
Add dry-run validation for row counts, IDs, settings keys, media references, and URL rewrites
Add media optimization, dedupe, and upload guardrails
Add quota monitoring and Telegram alerts
Create a Cloudflare/OpenNext deployment proof-of-concept while keeping Vercel live as fallback
Do not switch live traffic until legacy data export and validation pass
postRestoreMigration: Confirm Supabase REST and Storage APIs are available
Export DB data and Storage manifest
Copy referenced Supabase Storage objects to R2
Rewrite article body and thumbnail URLs
Run count, sample render, search, admin, upload, auto-press, Telegram, and maintenance-mode smoke tests
Deploy Cloudflare staging with D1/R2 bindings
Switch DNS only after Cloudflare and Vercel render counts match
Keep Supabase read-only and Vercel deployment available for 7-14 days
Clean or archive Supabase Storage after verification
